substep.title: Detect Mode
substep.action: Check whether the question tool is available. Available → INTERACTIVE. Absent → AUTONOMOUS. Later substeps branch on this.

check.action:
Confirm precedent_for is present. If absent, emit to stdout and stop:
ERROR: Missing required field 'precedent_for'. The dispatching caller failed to supply what we are seeking precedent for. No hunt performed.
Confirm what we are seeking precedent FOR — the problem, pattern, or capability. If genuinely ambiguous, ask one clarifying question via the question tool, recommended interpretation first.

substep.title: Read Mentioned Sources First
substep.action: If the subject names specific files or artifacts, read them FULLY now (no offset/limit). Knowing what WE are doing sharpens the match/differ assessment against any precedent found.

substep.title: Plan the Source Hunts
substep.action:
Decompose into precedent hunts across the three sources, each a focused question routed per references/research/child-subagent-dispatch.md: - Internal code precedent: "is there an existing implementation of {pattern} we could model after?" → codebase-pattern-finder - Prior-decision precedent: "have we planned or decided something like {subject} before?" → thoughts-locator (then thoughts-analyzer) - External precedent: "what open-source projects or documented patterns solve {subject}?" → web-search-researcher
Drop a source only when it cannot apply (e.g., no thoughts/ archive in this project). Most hunts run all three.

substep.title: Scope Gate
substep.action:
A precedent hunt targets one problem or pattern. If precedent_for is so broad it would survey many unrelated problems ("find all precedent for our architecture"), emit to stdout and stop:
SCOPE_TOO_LARGE Reason: subject spans many unrelated problems Suggested narrower hunts: - -
